All Work / Project 09 of 09
FLYP Creative Studio · Harappa · upGrad Enterprise
Brand Identity Pitch Design Motion Studio Systems

FLYP Creative
Studio System

A pitch deck and studio identity system for FLYP — Harappa's in-house creative studio — designed to communicate its creative range, value proposition, and working approach to prospective clients through a single, confident visual narrative.

Client
FLYP Creative Studio
Harappa · upGrad Enterprise
Deliverable
Pitch Deck
Studio Identity System
Year
2021
~30 hours
Tools
After Effects · Figma
Photoshop
Case Study
Context & Challenge

A creative studio that couldn't yet show what it was.

FLYP was Harappa's in-house creative studio — a team with genuine capability across motion, illustration, editorial, and interactive design. But without a coherent way to present itself externally, that capability was invisible to prospective clients. The studio had no single document that captured its thinking, its range, or its working approach in a form that could be shared and understood quickly.

The challenge was to build that document from scratch — a pitch deck that wasn't just a portfolio but a full studio identity and narrative system: coherent, visually confident, and structured to communicate FLYP's value proposition at the senior stakeholder level.

Objective

One deck that sells the whole studio.

To design a pitch deck and studio system that communicated FLYP's creative scope, philosophy, and past work in a single, unified visual narrative — persuasive enough to position the studio as a credible creative partner, and structured clearly enough to be understood and remembered by clients without a design background.

My Role
  • Shaped the narrative structure — sequencing the studio's proposition, creative approach, service offering, and case work into a logical, persuasive arc
  • Developed the visual language for the pitch system — typography, colour, layout principles, and the grid system that carried across every slide
  • Designed the interaction logic — transitions, information reveal sequencing, and the flow between sections for live presentation contexts
  • Produced motion elements in After Effects — animated sequences and transitions that gave the deck kinetic energy without competing with its content
  • Built the system in Figma — creating a scalable framework that the studio team could update and extend independently after handoff
  • Designed the overall studio identity — logomark, typographic system, and brand language that anchored the pitch deck within FLYP's broader presence
Adobe After Effects Figma Photoshop Motion Design Narrative Design
Impact

FLYP, clearly positioned.

The final deck gave FLYP a credible, confident way to present itself to prospective clients — clearly articulating the studio's creative capabilities and working approach in a form that could hold up in a boardroom. It positioned FLYP as a serious creative partner, not just an internal resource, and provided the studio with a scalable visual system it could build on beyond the initial deliverable.

Figma system overview — the complete pitch deck laid out across slides, showing the visual system, narrative arc, and the colour-coded section structure (black / yellow / red / teal / green) that gave each part of the studio story a distinct identity.

Opening title sequence — the FLYP brand mark emerging over the studio content grid, setting cinematic register from the first frame.

Motion logic — the logo letterforms flipping and reversing through the animation, creating kinetic energy that referenced the studio's "flipping" identity.

The Essence of FLYP — the studio's four-question content test (Fresh / Lasting Impact / You / Performance), each letter of FLYP anchoring one principle. A rare case where the brand acronym earns its existence structurally, not decoratively.

Case study 1: Insight to Action — the film-strip slide format presenting brief and FLYP's creative response, with embedded video preview integrated into the layout system.

Case study 3: Thrive Cast — the same slide architecture applied to a different project type, demonstrating how the layout system scaled across varied work without losing visual coherence.

Team FLYP — the studio's creative roster presented through colour-graded portrait photography, each team member given a distinct hue. A visual system that communicated the studio's cinematic production capability through the slide itself — not just through the work it described.

What the system covered
01

Narrative & Pitch Architecture

Structuring the studio's story — proposition, approach, service offering, and case work — into a persuasive arc that worked for both live presentation and standalone reading. Every section sequenced to build confidence in FLYP as a creative partner.

02

Visual Identity & Layout System

Designing the studio's visual language from the ground up — logomark, typographic hierarchy, colour system, and the grid and layout principles that carried consistently across every slide. Building it in Figma as a scalable system the team could extend independently.

03

Motion & Interaction Design

Producing animated sequences and transition logic in After Effects — giving the deck kinetic presence for live presentation contexts without competing with its content. Designing the interaction flow so the presentation felt curated, not mechanical.

A pitch deck is only as strong as its narrative — the design is what makes the argument feel inevitable.

— Design approach, FLYP Creative Studio System, Harappa · upGrad Enterprise, 2021

Process
01
Studio Audit & Narrative

Understanding FLYP's actual capabilities, past work, and the client contexts it was pitching into. Establishing the narrative arc — what the studio needed to say, in what order, and at what level of detail — before any visual decisions were made. Treating the pitch as a persuasion problem first, a design problem second.

02
Identity & Visual System

Designing the FLYP studio identity — logomark, typographic system, colour palette — and then building the pitch deck's layout system on top of it. Creating a visual language that felt confident and distinctive without overshadowing the work it was presenting. Building the entire system in Figma for team editability post-handoff.

03
Deck Design & Interaction

Laying out the full pitch deck — cover, proposition, service offering, methodology, case work, and closing — applying the visual system consistently across every section. Designing the interaction logic: information reveal sequencing, section transitions, and the flow between narrative beats for live presentation contexts.

04
Motion Production

Producing the animated elements in After Effects — title sequences, slide transitions, and kinetic typographic moments that gave the presentation its live energy. Calibrating the motion to feel purposeful rather than decorative: every animated element earning its place by making the narrative clearer or the pacing sharper.

What this work demonstrates

Narrative design for high-stakes pitches

Building a pitch deck isn't a layout exercise — it's a narrative and persuasion challenge with a visual solution. Structuring a studio's proposition into a sequence that builds trust, communicates range, and closes with confidence requires understanding both what a client needs to feel and what order they need to feel it in. This project demonstrates that specific competence.

Full-stack creative delivery

Narrative structure, visual identity, layout system, interaction design, and motion — all produced within a single 30-hour engagement. The breadth here is the point: demonstrating the ability to hold every dimension of a creative project simultaneously, without handing off any discipline to a specialist, and without any dimension suffering as a result.

Designing for the pitch context

A pitch deck lives in a live room, not on a screen — it has to work under pressure, guide a conversation, and survive a stakeholder who is half-listening and half-reading. Designing for that context means thinking about pacing, scannability, and the relationship between spoken narrative and visual content. This project demonstrates fluency in that specific, demanding design problem.
